summary: Split the lbshadow assembly range out of the larger lb assembly blob. The PR created asm/melee/lb/lbshadow.s for the 0x8000ED54-0x8000F9F4 text range and its related data strings/tables, renamed the remaining blob from code_8000E9F0.s to code_8000F9F8.s with its section starting at 0x8000F9F8, updated obj_files.mk to link lbshadow.o before code_8000F9F8.o, and changed MWCC_LD_VERSION from 1.3.2 to 1.1. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so intent beyond the mechanical split is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Added asm/melee/lb/lbshadow.s as a new assembly file containing the former leading portion of asm/melee/lb/code_8000E9F0.s.
The new lbshadow.s text section is explicitly marked as 0x8000ED54 - 0x8000F9F4.
Renamed the residual assembly blob from asm/melee/lb/code_8000E9F0.s to asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s.
Updated the residual blob section comment to begin at 0x8000F9F8 and continue to 0x80016158.
Moved lbshadow-associated data from the residual blob into lbshadow.s, including strings such as "lbshadow.c", "lbshadow", "shadow.h", and "coudn t get light position ...\n".
Updated obj_files.mk by replacing code_8000E9F0.o with two objects: lbshadow.o followed by code_8000F9F8.o.
Changed Makefile linker version setting from MWCC_LD_VERSION := 1.3.2 to MWCC_LD_VERSION := 1.1.
smart_moves: Preserved object order in obj_files.mk by inserting lbshadow.o immediately before code_8000F9F8.o, matching the original contiguous address order after the split.
Renamed the leftover generic code blob by its new starting address, code_8000F9F8.s, which keeps address-based asm blob names accurate after extracting a named subsystem.
Moved the related .data labels with the lbshadow code instead of leaving lbshadow strings and tables in the following generic blob.
Kept the extracted file assembly-only and mechanically equivalent, minimizing semantic risk while improving future decompilation boundaries.
decomp_lessons: When a known source filename string such as "lbshadow.c" appears inside a broad asm blob, it is strong evidence for a natural split boundary and a future C translation unit name.
For assembly blob splits, update both the source path and object list; the build must link the newly split object in the same relative order as the original address range.
After extracting a leading range from an address-named asm file, rename the remaining file to its new first function/address to keep navigation and search consistent.
Data near a split boundary should be audited for ownership; this PR moved the lbshadow data labels with the lbshadow text range.
A linker version change can be part of a matching/build-layout fix, but this slice does not explain why MWCC_LD_VERSION 1.1 was required.
naming_conventions: Named subsystem asm files under asm/melee/lb use lower-case module names, e.g. asm/melee/lb/lbshadow.s.
Unidentified residual asm blobs are named by their starting address, e.g. code_8000F9F8.s.
Object file entries mirror assembly filenames under $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/lb/, e.g. lbshadow.o and code_8000F9F8.o.
Existing unidentified function and label names were preserved in the split, e.g. func_8000ED54, func_8000F214, lbl_803BA0D8, and lbl_803BA0FC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use address comments in .section lines to document exact extracted ranges: lbshadow.s covers 0x8000ED54 - 0x8000F9F4, while code_8000F9F8.s starts at 0x8000F9F8.
Keep the extracted assembly body byte-for-byte style intact, including .global declarations, labels, instruction comments, and .data entries.
Split the original object into two linked objects at a function boundary, then list them consecutively in obj_files.mk to preserve layout.
Move source-identifying strings and related tables into the new module so later C decompilation has the correct data co-located with its functions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/melee/lb/lbshadow.s to C.
Search for lbshadow.c and shadow.h strings in later decompilation work.
Look for PRs explaining why MWCC_LD_VERSION was changed from 1.3.2 to 1.1.
Search later symbol maps for renamed versions of func_8000ED54 through func_8000F214.
